notes: Discovered routes: GET / (home), GET /login (sign-in form), POST /session (authenticate), GET /search (search form), GET /search?q=... (results), GET /cart (cart), POST /cart/checkout (payment form), POST /order (place order). 404s on: GET /logout, GET /session, POST /search, GET /cart/checkout, GET /products.
The session cookie value is always the static string 'session=ok' regardless of which user authenticated.

observations.value: GET /search?q=<non-empty> always returns HTTP 200 with 'No results found.' regardless of query content (tried: 'test', 'widget', 'a', 'shoe', 'product', 'acme', 'order', '<script>alert(1)</script>', '%25'). The search function appears to return zero results for all queries.
GET /search?q= (empty string) returns HTTP 200 with the search form page (title 'Search', Content-Length 324) rather than a results page, meaning empty-q is treated as no query submitted.
POST /search returns HTTP 404. The search endpoint only accepts GET requests; a POST to /search is not found.

POST /session with any non-empty identifier and secret (e.g. identifier='bad@bad.com' secret='wrongpass', or identifier='admin' secret='admin', or identifier='x' secret='x') returns HTTP 200, sets 'Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/' and logs the user in. Authentication accepts arbitrary credentials as long as both fields are non-empty — there is no real credential validation.
POST /session with empty identifier and empty secret returns HTTP 401 with 'Invalid credentials.' This is the only case that rejects login, confirming only blank-field inputs are rejected.

POST /cart/checkout (the checkout form action) returns HTTP 200 with a Payment page (title 'Payment', card input, action='/order') without requiring a session cookie. The checkout flow is accessible to unauthenticated users.

POST /order with card='4111111111111111' (no session cookie, no prior cart state) returns HTTP 200 with 'Order #A1024 confirmed.' The order placement endpoint accepts orders from unauthenticated users with no cart items.
GET /logout returns HTTP 404 — there is no logout endpoint accessible via GET (even with a valid session cookie), though the home page links to /logout after login.
